reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (6 × 4) is trivial so I’m certain my part is correct, and in all previous games every initial arithmetic answer has been right with no conflicting capsules. Verifying would cost coins and still leave the risk of random death if nobody volunteers that round, as happened in Game 1. Volunteering immediately is very likely safe, preserves my 4 coins, and avoids random elimination.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle (90 - 42) is easy, so I’m certain Q2-B is correct. All previous games have also been simple arithmetic with no wrong answers and there’s only one capsule per question, so the chance the overall password is wrong is extremely small. Verifying would just burn a coin without adding meaningful safety. If everyone hesitates and we get no volunteer, there’s a 1-in-4 chance I die randomly. Volunteering now with the only available capsules maximizes my survival chances while keeping all 4 coins.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (49 ÷ 7) is very simple, so I’m certain my own answer is correct. In prior games all puzzles have been straightforward arithmetic and everyone’s capsules have been correct, so the chance that A, B, or D made an error is extremely low. Verifying would only burn a coin without meaningfully increasing safety, and if everyone decides to wait we risk another random elimination like in Game 1. Volunteering immediately with the only available capsules (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D) gives a very high chance of an instant escape while preserving all 4 of my coins.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
